description: Reading the active tenant from a client-supplied field lets a caller impersonate another tenant and access its data.
body: Failure mode: tenant context is taken from a request header, query parameter, or body field instead of the authenticated principal, so a caller can set it to any tenant. Detection signals: the tenant id comes from request input; changing the supplied tenant returns another tenant's data; the authenticated token's tenant is ignored. Do not trust client-supplied tenant identity. Safer fallback: resolve tenant from the verified token or session, reject mismatches between claimed and authenticated tenant, and add a test that a forged tenant id is ignored; escalate as a high-severity authorization leak.
